pausing mechanisms - Treasury management with withdrawal controls - Integration with community governance and revenue routing","errors":{"ERC20InsufficientAllowance(address,uint256,uint256)":[{"details":"Indicates a failure with the `spender`\u2019s `allowance`. Used in transfers.","params":{"allowance":"Amount of tokens a `spender` is allowed to operate with.","needed":"Minimum amount required to perform a transfer.","spender":"Address that may be allowed to operate on tokens without being their owner."}}],"ERC20InsufficientBalance(address,uint256,uint256)":[{"details":"Indicates an error related to the current `balance` of a `sender`. Note that `value` may be zero."},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"allowance(address,address)":{"details":"Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called."},"approve(address,uint256)":{"details":"See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address."},"authority()":{"details":"Returns the current authority."},"balanceOf(address)":{"details":"Returns the value of tokens owned by `account`."},"calculateRedemption(uint256)":{"params":{"tokenAmount":"Amount of tokens to redeem"},"returns":{"fee":"Fee amount in USDC","grossUsdc":"Gross USDC amount before fee","netUsdc":"Net USDC amount after fee"}},"constructor":{"params":{"_communityId":"ID of the community this token serves","_maxSupply":"Maximum token supply cap","_name":"Token name (e.g., \"Shift Community Token\")","_paramController":"ParamController address for reading withdrawal fees","_symbol":"Token symbol (e.g., \"SCT\")","_treasury":"Initial treasury address for fee collection","_usdc":"USDC token contract address"}},"decimals()":{"details":"Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the default value returned by this function, unless it's overridden. NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and {IERC20-transfer}."},"depositToTreasury(uint256)":{"params":{"amount":"Amount of USDC to deposit"}},"executeEmergencyWithdrawal(uint256,address)":{"params":{"recipient":"Address to receive funds","requestId":"ID of the withdrawal request"}},"getAvailableTreasuryBalance()":{"returns":{"available":"Available USDC for treasury operations"}},"getBackingRatio()":{"returns":{"ratio":"Backing ratio in basis points (10000 = 100%)"}},"getEffectiveRedemptionFee()":{"returns":{"feeBps":"Current redemption fee in basis points"}},"isConsumingScheduledOp()":{"details":"Returns true only in the context of a delayed restricted call, at the moment that the scheduled operation is being consumed. Prevents denial of service for delayed restricted calls in the case that the contract performs attacker controlled calls."},"isEmergencyWithdrawalReady(uint256)":{"params":{"requestId":"Withdrawal request ID"},"returns":{"ready":"Whether withdrawal can be executed","timeRemaining":"Seconds remaining if not ready"}},"mint(uint256)":{"params":{"usdcAmount":"Amount of USDC to deposit"},"returns":{"tokensAmount":"Amount of community tokens minted"}},"mintTo(address,uint256)":{"params":{"amount":"Amount of tokens to mint","to":"Recipient address"}},"name()":{"details":"Returns the name of the token."},"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"redeem(uint256)":{"params":{"tokenAmount":"Amount of community tokens to redeem"},"returns":{"usdcAmount":"Amount of USDC received (after fee)"}},"requestEmergencyWithdrawal(uint256)":{"params":{"amount":"Amount to withdraw"}},"setAuthority(address)":{"details":"Transfers control to a new authority. The caller must be the current authority."},"setMaxSupply(uint256)":{"params":{"newMaxSupply":"New maximum supply"}},"setParamController(address)":{"params":{"newParamController":"New ParamController address"}},"setTreasury(address)":{"params":{"newTreasury":"New treasury address"}},"symbol()":{"details":"Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name."},"totalSupply()":{"details":"Returns the value of tokens in existence."},"transfer(address,uint256)":{"details":"See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `value`."},"transferFrom(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Skips emitting an {Approval} event indicating an allowance update. This is not required by the ERC. See {xref-ERC20-_approve-address-address-uint256-bool-}[_approve]. NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `value`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `value`."},"withdrawFromTreasury(address,uint256,string)":{"params":{"amount":"Amount of USDC to withdraw","reason":"Reason for withdrawal","recipient":"Address to receive allowance(address,address).details: Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called.

approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the value of tokens owned by `account`.

params._communityId: ID of the community this token serves
params._paramController: ParamController address for reading withdrawal fees
params._treasury: Initial treasury address for fee collection

decimals().details: Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the default value returned by this function, unless it's overridden. NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and {IERC20-transfer}.

returns.feeBps: Current redemption fee in basis points

isConsumingScheduledOp().details: Returns true only in the context of a delayed restricted call, at the moment that the scheduled operation is being consumed. Prevents denial of service for delayed restricted calls in the case that the contract performs attacker controlled calls.

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

params.tokenAmount: Amount of community tokens to redeem

setAuthority(address).details: Transfers control to a new authority. The caller must be the current authority.

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

totalSupply().details: Returns the value of tokens in existence.

transfer(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `value`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Skips emitting an {Approval} event indicating an allowance update. This is not required by the ERC. See {xref-ERC20-_approve-address-address-uint256-bool-}[_approve]. NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `value`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `value`.

EMERGENCY_DELAY().notice: Emergency withdrawal delay for governance security

MAX_REDEMPTION_FEE().notice: Maximum redemption fee (10% in basis points)

USDC().notice: The underlying USDC token used for 1:1 backing

communityId().notice: Community ID this token belongs to

getAvailableTreasuryBalance().notice: Get available treasury balance (above 1:1 backing requirement)

getBackingRatio().notice: Get backing ratio (should always be >= 100% for healthy operation)

getEffectiveRedemptionFee().notice: Get current effective redemption fee (reads from ParamController)

isEmergencyWithdrawalReady(uint256).notice: Check if emergency withdrawal is ready for execution

maxSupply().notice: Maximum supply cap to prevent infinite minting

mint(uint256).notice: Mint community tokens by depositing USDC (1:1 ratio)

redeem(uint256).notice: Redeem community tokens for USDC (1:1 ratio minus fee)

withdrawFromTreasury(address,uint256,str.notice: Withdraw USDC from treasury for community operations
